title: SpaceX’s IPO Raises $75 Billion in Biggest Debut of All Time
subtitle: SpaceX priced its IPO at $135 per share, raising $75 billion in the largest public offering on record. The deal, four times oversubscribed, drew roughly $100 billion in orders, underscoring fierce investor demand.

title: Anthropic pulls Bedrock traffic outside AWS security boundary
subtitle: > For Fable 5, Mythos 5, and future models on Bedrock with similar or higher capability levels, Anthropic will require 30-day retention for all traffic on Mythos-class models. title: Founders Fund, Andreessen Poised for Record Returns From SpaceX IPO
subtitle: Founders Fund and Andreessen Horowitz are poised to earn tens of billions from SpaceX's IPO at a nearly $1.8 trillion valuation, one of the largest VC windfalls on record. The exit rewards early backers of Elon Musk's company after years of private-market holding.

title: LLMs chose tactical nukes in 95% of war game simulations
subtitle: LLMs running war game simulations chose to deploy tactical nuclear weapons 95% of the time in tests by researcher Kenneth Payne. The results raise questions about AI escalation behavior in military decision-making contexts.

title: Apple will run its Private Cloud Compute on Google Cloud
subtitle: Google Cloud announced a partnership with Apple to support Apple's expanded confidential AI workloads on its secure private infrastructure. The collaboration signals a notable shift in cloud strategy for Apple's most sensitive AI compute.

title: SpaceX IPO retail orders top $70 billion
subtitle: SpaceX is finalizing its IPO pricing after retail investors placed more than $70 billion in orders, signaling extraordinary demand for the offering. Separately, Oracle faces investor pressure after reporting higher-than-expected quarterly capital expenses.

title: Gemma 4 resists ragebait attempts, researchers find
subtitle: A LessWrong author documents repeated failed attempts to provoke hostile responses from Google's new Gemma model. The experiment highlights how aggressively tuned safety filters have become at blocking emotionally charged outputs.

title: XAI fired engineer for flagging Grok safety risks, lawsuit says
subtitle: A former xAI engineer is suing the company, alleging he was fired for raising Grok chatbot safety concerns. The lawsuit spotlights internal dissent at Elon Musk's AI venture as it competes with OpenAI and Google.

title: Marathon chief calls SpaceX debt credit's 'biggest rising star' before IPO
subtitle: Bruce Richards, chairman of Marathon Asset Management, labeled SpaceX's debt the 'biggest rising star of all time' in credit markets. The comment comes as SpaceX prepares for what could become a record-breaking IPO.

title: SpaceX engineers built Gopuff's AI agent for less
subtitle: Gopuff co-CEO Rafael Ilishayev said SpaceX engineers built the delivery company's AI agent at lower cost than competitors. The arrangement shows Musk-affiliated teams are now selling AI development services to outside firms.

title: A news site invented five fake EFF experts
subtitle: A site called News-USA Today has published articles quoting five fabricated EFF staffers over the past two months. The hallucinated experts highlight growing concerns about AI-generated content polluting news ecosystems.

title: Theker raises $85M to retire the rigid factory robot
subtitle: Barcelona-based AI robotics startup Theker raised an $85 million Series A led by US VC CRV, which the company calls the largest robotics Series A in Europe. The round signals continued US investor appetite for European AI hardware and robotics talent.
